executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 295,137.76 inc VAT (268,578.86 ex VAT) for the week ending 06 June 2026.
Overall gross margin came in at 18.37%, which requires close monitoring given the level of waste recorded across multiple departments.
Total waste for the week was 11,524.10, representing 4.29% of sales ex VAT - this is a significant drag on profitability and is the primary concern this week.

The fresh departments - Poultry (1,997.00), Deli (1,779.82), Provisions (1,282.00), Fish (1,182.89) and Produce (1,444.38) - are the five largest waste contributors, collectively accounting for 7,686.09 or 66.7% of total waste.

Several departments are reporting P&L figures that appear structurally incomplete - missing purchases, opening stock or COGS data - meaning the reported margins of 100%+ in multiple departments are not reliable for management decision-making this week.

Departments where closing stock has been captured without corresponding purchases data (Beef, Lamb, Pork, Bacon, Poultry, Fish, Provisions) are generating negative COGS entries, which is artificially inflating reported profit figures.
Scan margin data indicates Deli is the strongest theoretical margin department at 71.71%, while Cigarettes (9.51%), Fish (13.01%) and Bacon (12.79%) are the lowest scan margin performers.
Bread and Cakes waste of 1,155.20 and Dairy waste of 847.48 are notable and suggest ordering and rotation disciplines need review in those categories.
biggest_wins: Deli recorded sales of 11,272.94 with a scan margin of 71.71%, making it the highest theoretical margin department in the store and a strong contributor to overall profitability.
Produce delivered the highest sales volume among the fresh categories at 25,009.02, with a scan margin of 20.54%, indicating solid ranging and throughput performance.
Provisions posted sales of 17,624.44 with a scan margin of 20.99%, representing a strong mid-range category by both volume and theoretical margin.

Beef was the second highest fresh department by sales at 9,958.34 and carried a scan margin of 16.77%, maintaining a reasonable contribution despite being the third highest waste department at 525.15.
Poultry generated sales of 7,625.05 with a scan margin of 16.13%, though this must be viewed in the context of its 1,997.00 waste figure which significantly erodes the net return.
biggest_risks: Poultry waste stands at 1,997.00 against sales of 7,625.05, implying waste as a percentage of Poultry sales of approximately 26.2% - this is an acute risk and requires immediate review of ordering volumes, shelf life management and promotional activity.

Deli waste of 1,779.82 against sales of 11,272.94 represents approximately 15.8% waste-to-sales, which is high even for a food-to-go department and points to production over-ordering or end-of-day write-off issues.

Provisions waste of 1,282.00 is unexpectedly high for what is largely an ambient/chilled category with longer shelf life; root cause investigation is required to determine whether this is date-code clearance, damage or ordering error.

Fish waste of 1,182.89 against sales of 2,337.87 equates to approximately 50.6% waste-to-sales, which is an extremely elevated ratio and suggests the Fish counter is significantly over-stocked relative to current customer demand.

Produce waste of 1,444.38 against sales of 25,009.02 is approximately 5.8% waste-to-sales; while more proportionate than other departments, the absolute value is material and seasonal ranging should be reviewed.

Bread and Cakes waste of 1,155.20 is a significant figure and, given it does not appear in the department P&L above with its own scan margin line, its contribution to margin erosion may be underappreciated in weekly reviews.

Multiple departments (Beef, Lamb, Pork, Bacon, Poultry, Fish, Provisions) have no purchases or opening stock data recorded this week, meaning COGS is calculated purely as negative closing stock. This renders the P&L for these departments unreliable and a data integrity issue must be addressed with the bookkeeping or ordering system.

Bacon and Loose S.R.P. is showing a reported margin of 207.64% due to the missing purchases data issue; its scan margin is only 12.79%, one of the lowest in the store, meaning real-world profitability is likely under pressure and the true P&L position needs to be established.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Sales of 10,806.54 recorded with a scan margin of 9.51%. No purchases, opening or closing stock data present; reported margin of 100% is a data artefact. Scan margin of 9.51% reflects the expected low-margin, high-volume nature of the category.

Beef: Sales of 9,958.34 with scan margin of 16.77%. Closing stock of 6,600.05 recorded but no purchases data, producing a negative COGS of -6,600.05 and an artificial margin of 166.28%. Waste of 525.15 needs monitoring. P&L data must be completed.

Lamb: Sales of 964.73 with scan margin of 26.09%, the highest scan margin among the meat departments. Closing stock of 635.54 with no purchases data creates negative COGS and an unreliable reported margin of 165.88%. Waste of 151.56 is proportionate to sales.

Pork: Sales of 1,342.21 with scan margin of 17.33%. Same missing purchases data issue resulting in negative COGS of -969.93. Waste of 254.62 represents approximately 19.0% of sales, which warrants a review of ordering levels.

Bacon and Loose S.R.P.: Sales of 1,118.46 with the lowest scan margin in the meat group at 12.79%. Missing purchases data inflates reported margin to 207.64%. Waste of 130.28 is notable relative to the sales base.

Poultry: Sales of 7,625.05 with scan margin of 16.13%. Waste of 1,997.00 is the single largest waste line in the store this week and represents the most urgent operational issue. Purchases data absent, making the reported 144.53% margin unreliable.

Fish: Sales of 2,337.87 with scan margin of 13.01%. Waste of 1,182.89 is disproportionately high relative to sales and implies the counter is over-stocked. Reported margin of 146.63% is a data artefact due to missing purchases.

Produce: Sales of 25,009.02 with scan margin of 20.54%. No purchases or stock data available; reported margin of 100% is a data artefact. Waste of 1,444.38 is the second highest in absolute terms and requires ongoing management through ordering discipline and markdown activity.

Deli: Sales of 11,272.94 with the strongest scan margin in the store at 71.71%. No stock or purchases data recorded. Waste of 1,779.82 is high and reducing production over-runs should be a priority to protect the inherent margin strength of this department.

Provisions: Sales of 17,624.44 with scan margin of 20.99%. Closing stock of only 23.33 recorded with no purchases data, resulting in a marginal COGS distortion. Waste of 1,282.00 is an outlier for this category type and requires investigation.
action_plan: Investigate and resolve the missing purchases and opening stock data for Beef, Lamb, Pork, Bacon, Poultry, Fish and Provisions immediately - without complete COGS data these departments cannot be managed on P&L, and the issue must be corrected before next week's report is produced.

Conduct an urgent review of Poultry ordering and production volumes; with waste of 1,997.00 against sales of 7,625.05, the department manager must present a revised order schedule and markdown plan to the store manager by mid-week.

Initiate a Fish counter sales and waste review - waste of 1,182.89 against sales of 2,337.87 is not commercially sustainable. Assess whether counter hours, ranging or display volume should be reduced to align supply with actual demand.

Review Deli production schedules for each day part; waste of 1,779.82 at a 71.71% scan margin means that every unit wasted carries a high opportunity cost. Implement a daily end-of-production waste log and compare against production sheets.

Investigate the Provisions waste figure of 1,282.00 - identify whether this relates to specific product lines, date-code clearance, damage or administrative write-offs, and address the root cause before the next ordering cycle.
Implement a daily markdown programme across Produce, Poultry, Fish and Deli to convert impending waste into sales and recover margin rather than writing product off at full cost.

Review Bread and Cakes ordering against sales data; waste of 1,155.20 suggests orders are not sufficiently aligned to sales patterns. Work with the supplier representative to refine delivery quantities, particularly for weekend and early-week days.

Audit Dairy waste of 847.48 - review date rotation compliance on the shop floor and in the back-up cooler, and verify that stock rotation (FIFO) procedures are being followed consistently by the team.

Schedule a full Bacon and Loose S.R.P. margin review given its scan margin of 12.79% is among the lowest in the building; confirm that all products are correctly linked in the system and that promotions are being accounted for accurately in the scan data.
Ensure all department managers submit complete opening stock, closing stock and purchases figures for the coming week to allow accurate COGS and GP% calculation across all categories.
Review Pork waste of 254.62 relative to sales of 1,342.21 (approximately 19.0% waste-to-sales) and implement a targeted order reduction or inter-department transfer plan to reduce exposure.

Present a consolidated waste reduction target to all department managers for the coming week, with a store-level goal of reducing total waste below 3.50% of sales, from the current 4.29%, as a step toward protecting overall store margin.
